How to deal with Significant Environmental aspects in ISO 14001

January 2, 2026general

In any ISO 14001 EMS certified organization, distinguishing and addressing significant environmental aspects is essential to performance and success. How does

How to deal with Significant Environmental aspects in ISO 14001

In any ISO 14001 EMS (Environmental Management System) certified organization or company, distinguishing and addressing significant environmental aspects is essential to performance, and so, to the success of both system and environment. With legislation, and concerns relating to your organization's employees, customers, shareholders, stakeholders and therefore the local community, there'll be several things to contemplate when dealing with your environmental aspect and distinguishing what aspects could also be more or less important. Therefore, it is necessary that when you establish an environmental aspect that's particularly vital to your organization, and most importantly you should have a substantial methodology or strategies to addressing these important environmental aspects that alleviate, improves, or even get rid of the impact it has on the surroundings and environment as this is the prime motive of the ISO 14001 EMS certification standard. Therefore, how does one decide what environmental aspects are significantly vital, and what strategies one should be able to employ to address them?

Significant Environmental Aspects

When addressing with different types of environmental aspects, most of the time every organization will tend to rate or prioritize the chosen those environment aspects pertaining to ISO EMS on a scale of importance, as in aligning with any reasonably logical risk-based approach. So, therefore, it is an essential reason that the organization ought to establish aspects that could be a higher level of risk to the organization sooner or later, as opposed to those aspects which could result in risk either to a lesser extent or in the near future. Utilizing such a rating system will facilitate the organization sort out environmental aspects, and so you can efficiently choose that are more important than others.

For instance, manufacturing or industrial plant's emissions and packaging waste could both be known aspects for your organization and EMS, however, if your emissions are nearly exceeding the legal boundaries for your region, then it would be prudent to make a decision that this kind of impact to the surroundings and environment and your stakeholders is more immediate and serious than a small degree of waste management in line with ISO 14001 certification principles. Therefore, it could be sensible to contemplate the emissions issue as a major environmental risk, whereas the packaging issue may well be taken on a less serious note, and dealt later. If you don't use a substantial classification system to classify your organization’s significant environmental aspects against those are less important, it might be wise to do.

How to deal with these significant enviroment aspects?

Now that we have established a way to determine a major environmental aspect, it is sensible to ascertain a method to make sure that they're dealt with efficiency. Provided that failure to mitigate or remove a major environmental aspect could mean that objectives don't seem to be earned and continual improvement isn't evident, it's clear to envision that incorrect management of serious environmental aspects could be a risk to the performance of the EMS. It is recommended to seek the help of ISO certification consultants so that they will let us examine some strategies of addressing this scenario:

Always make sure that your important environmental aspects are reviewed, assessed and monitored at your regular organization's monthly or quarterly meetings. This is often very rudimental approach, but always a quite significant when it comes to classifying them to address them

Have a “brain dump” and ask your most responsible organizational leaders to assist in identifying an answer to solve your most significant environmental aspects – some contemporary mental capacity could help to uncover a solution you don’t already see. Involve either your organization’s leaders or ISO consultants at the beginning itself. Because this level of consultation will guarantee and assist you to notice solutions which are then left to take into consideration before.

Delegate a project team A major environmental aspect could often be treated equally to the other project. By delegating a project manager, strategies of action, assessment, review, and responsibilities, you will be able to make sure that all major environmental aspects get the attention it needs, and “project creep” will not become an issue.

Record all information accurately:This is often crucial for various of reasons – to make sure you are reviewing and analyzing actions and progressing accurately, to make sure that a record exists in your organization's database which can be used later for audit and future reference functions, and to demonstrate that you simply are achieving the continual improvement as far as ISO 14001 EMS certification standard is concerned about. All these continual improvements should also be recorded on your environmental aspect database, where the aspects would have been recorded at the beginning.

Set a review date: Make sure that you set a review date to assess a major environmental aspect. Even though you are certainly sure the primary cause has been taken care and removed, it's typically a sensible approach to make sure that after a certain amount of time there has been no reoccurrence is recorded. In some cases, this could capture a reoccurrence at a low level, and guarantee to any extent further corrective action is of a minor nature, which can not only save your organization work and resource within the short term but also defend your organization in the long term where stakeholders are involved.

Communicate effectively: Regardless of communicating internally or externally, correct and clear flow of data is significant. Stakeholders will usually have an interest in how you take care of important environmental aspects, particularly when it involves legislation problems, therefore communicating clearly provides them with the prospect to visualize what your organization’s EMS is achieving. This additionally opens the feedback and will enable suggestions that allow you to boost your processes consequently

How to deal with Significant Environmental aspects in ISO 14001
